marketState: neutral, cross-currents: near-term bullish weather vs. longer-term bearish storage/technicals
summary: Price ($2.88) sits essentially at modeled fair value ($2.923, band $2.423-$3.423) and about 8.5pp below its seasonal median, but seasonality reliability is low this week (59pp 10-90% spread) so that edge is weak. The standout signal is weather: 8-16 day cooling demand is +36.8% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max (rangePos 1.18), a real near-term bullish catalyst even though 24h demand momentum just ticked down. Working against that near-term read is a bearish setup further out - storage carries a +180 Bcf surplus vs the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and every technical timeframe from 5m to 1w is bearish with a 50<200 death cross. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, a stretched positioning that raises short-covering risk and partially offsets the bearish storage/technical picture. Net effect: a modest near-term long case on the weather catalyst, but a genuinely mixed multi-week picture that argues for patience rather than a fresh directional bet.

longTerm.rationale: Storage's +180 Bcf surplus vs 5y and bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, plus bearish daily/weekly technicals (RSI ~36, death cross), argue against a fresh long over a multi-week horizon. But managed money is net short at only the 8th percentile of 5 years - stretched positioning that raises short-covering risk - and fair value is drifting higher into the fall ($2.923 now to $3.044 by +21D) as the curve prices in a large winter premium (Dec $3.724, Jan $4.151). These signals roughly cancel, so no edge to act on yet.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) COT mmNet percentile to start climbing off single digits together with a bullish EMA cross on the daily/weekly timeframes - favors LONG, or (b) a confirmed break below ~$2.70 with the storage surplus continuing to widen - favors SHORT.

shortTerm.rationale: 8-16 day cooling demand is running +36.8% above the 5-year norm and above the historical max (rangePos 1.18), a genuine near-term bullish power-burn catalyst, while front-month price sits at/slightly under fair value ($2.88 vs $2.923) with the curve showing modest backwardation into September (tight prompt). Bearish technicals across every timeframe (death cross, bearish trend) argue against chasing strength, so this is a defined-risk entry on weakness rather than a breakout buy.
shortTerm.invalidation: Sustained close below $2.75, or the 8-16D demand forecast normalizing back toward its 5-year average, would remove the bullish catalyst and hand control back to the prevailing bearish technical trend.

keyDrivers: 8-16 day demand +36.8% vs 5y and above the historical max - the strongest near-term bullish catalyst
Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of 5 years - stretched positioning, contrarian short-covering risk
Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise - caps longer-term upside
All technical timeframes (5m-1w) bearish with 50<200 death cross - trend fighting the fundamentals
Price essentially at fair value ($2.88 vs $2.923) with fair value drifting higher into winter premium
risks: Seasonality reliability is low this week (59pp 10-90% spread), so the seasonal edge could easily fail
